# aigateway_cost_center (Resource)

A cost center (budget) on the gateway. Used to attribute API key usage and, optionally, cap daily/weekly/monthly spend. Sub-limits allow fine-grained caps per provider, model, alias or router within the budget.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "aigateway_cost_center" "companygpt" {
  name     = "companygpt"
  currency = "EUR"
  # no monthly_cap → attribution-only (unlimited)
}

resource "aigateway_cost_center" "customer_a" {
  name        = "customer-a"
  currency    = "EUR"
  mode        = "per_user"
  monthly_cap = "500.00"
  weekly_cap  = "200.00"
  daily_cap   = "50.00"

  auto_add_new_users = false
  fallback_chain     = [aigateway_cost_center.companygpt.id]

  sub_limits = [
    {
      scope_type = "provider"
      scope_id   = "provider_anthropic"
      cap_amount = "50.00"
      weekly_cap = "20.00"
    },
    {
      scope_type = "model"
      scope_id   = "gpt-5.4"
      cap_amount = "0" # blocked
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Cost center (budget) name, unique per tenant.

### Optional

- `agent_id` (String) Optional agent id this budget is scoped to.
- `auto_add_new_users` (Boolean) When true, users provisioned after this budget is created are auto-added as members.
- `daily_cap` (String) Daily cap as a decimal string. "0" blocks, a positive number caps, omit/null = unlimited (clearable).
- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `fallback_chain` (List of String) Ordered list of cost center (budget) ids to fall back to when this center is exhausted.
- `is_org` (Boolean) Mark as the org-level cost center.
- `monthly_cap` (String) Monthly cap as a decimal string (e.g. "500.00"); omit for unlimited (attribution-only).
- `sub_limits` (Block List) Fine-grained caps within this budget, scoped to a provider/model/alias/router. Reconciled against the gateway sub-limit sub-resources on every apply. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sub_limits))
- `weekly_cap` (String) Weekly cap as a decimal string. "0" blocks, a positive number caps, omit/null = unlimited (clearable). Must satisfy daily <= weekly <= monthly when set.

### Read-Only

- `currency` (String) ISO 4217 currency code. Defaults to USD.
- `id` (String) Server-assigned cost center (budget) uuid.
- `mode` (String) Budget mode: pool (one shared counter) | per_user (a counter per member). Defaults to pool.

<a id="nestedblock--sub_limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sub_limits`

Required:

- `cap_amount` (String) Monthly cap for this sub-limit as a decimal string. "0" blocks; must be <= the budget monthly_cap when that is set.
- `scope_type` (String) Scope dimension: provider | model | alias | router.

Optional:

- `alias_name` (String) The alias name for alias scope. Leave null for the other scopes.
- `daily_cap` (String) Optional daily cap for this sub-limit (decimal string). "0" blocks; null = unlimited.
- `scope_id` (String) The provider id / model id / router id for provider|model|router scopes. Leave null for alias scope.
- `weekly_cap` (String) Optional weekly cap for this sub-limit (decimal string). "0" blocks; null = unlimited.

## Cap tri-state semantics

Caps (`monthly_cap`, `weekly_cap`, `daily_cap`) are clearable:

- Omit or set to `null` → unlimited (no cap; gateway clears any previously-set cap)
- `"0"` → blocked (all requests rejected)
- `"50.00"` → capped at that decimal value

On update, the provider always sends an explicit `null` for cleared caps so the gateway's double-option PATCH path removes them. Do NOT set `UseStateForUnknown` on cap attributes — they must be clearable.
